## Audit 1/3 — 2026-04-19 01:30

### references/standards-registry.md

```yaml
file: references/standards-registry.md
section: full file
parser_consumer: p01_populations_references, p_standards_ext
format_summary: "YAML fenced blocks, one per jurisdiction-standard pair"
known_patterns:
  - entry_format: "fenced yaml block with 7 fields"
  - fields: "jurisdiction, standard_cited, current_version, status, key_changes, last_checked, source_url"
  - jurisdiction_codes: "2-letter ISO or org name (ISO, EU, UN)"
edge_cases:
  - "Template block ([2-letter code or full name]) included as entry — parser must skip"
  - "30 entries across 15 real jurisdictions; A5 target is 80+ entries"
  - "status field values: CURRENT, UPDATED, SUPERSEDED, WITHDRAWN"
audit_status: PASS
audit_date: 2026-04-19 01:30
standards_gap: "50+ entries needed to meet A5 target (80+ total from ~30 current)"
```

### parts/v10/part02.md (Part 2: Disability Categories)

```yaml
file: parts/v10/part02.md
section: full file (325 lines, 35517 chars)
parser_consumer: p02_populations
format_summary: "H3 per population (### §2.N CODE). Prose paragraphs. No H4 subsections."
known_patterns:
  - heading_format: "### §2.N CODE"
  - population_sections: 11
  - populations: "MOB, MOB/UPL, VIS, DEAF, NEU, DEM, NDV, NDV/MH, PAIN, DBL, OFS"
  - internal_structure: "Functional profile → Primary environmental barriers → Key design provisions → Evidence confidence → Primary BPC"
  - co1_evidence_gap_callouts: "blockquote at top of sections with [Co-1 EVIDENCE GAP]"
  - bpc_references: "Primary BPC: slug-name; slug-name"
  - evidence_confidence_ratings: "HIGH, LOW, etc. — informal inline text, not structured field"
edge_cases:
  - "No IntD section (proxied through DEM/NDV per project-standards)"
  - "Category Code Reference table at top (L15-30) uses pipe-delimited markdown table"
  - "No evidence tier markers (● / ○) present"
  - "Cross-references use §N.N format"
  - "Item code references sparse (17 total) — A-01 format"
  - "Evidence confidence is prose, not structured — parser must extract from text"
audit_status: PASS-WITH-NOTES
audit_date: 2026-04-19 01:30
notes: "Parser for p02_populations will need to extract structured fields from prose. No formal subsection pattern — content is in named bold paragraphs (**Functional profile.** etc). Consider whether parser reads prose or whether a pre-pass adds structured YAML front matter per section."
```

### parts/v10/part03.md — §3.3 (Co-Occurrence Matrix)

```yaml
file: parts/v10/part03.md
section: "§3.3 Co-Occurrence Matrix (L59-83)"
parser_consumer: p04_co_occurrence_matrix
format_summary: "Markdown table with 5 columns"
known_patterns:
  - table_columns: "Population A | Population B | Type | Primary conflict domain | Resolution status"
  - type_values: "BOTH, INTER-GROUP, INTRA-INDIVIDUAL"
  - resolution_status_values: "RESOLVED-CONSENSUS, RESOLVED-EVIDENCE, RESOLVED, PARTIAL"
  - conflict_domain_codes: "TEMP-RANGE, COLOUR-CONT, ACOUSTIC-LVL, SPATIAL-OPEN, SURFACE-TEXT, LIGHT-INT, MOVE-FREE, FRAGRANCE, VIS-COMPLEX"
  - row_count: 12
edge_cases:
  - "Some rows have multiple conflict domains comma-separated (e.g. MOVE-FREE, COLOUR-CONT)"
  - "Resolution status includes cross-reference (— see Part 5 §5.2)"
  - "Introductory prose before table contains CO-0003 terminology note"
audit_status: PASS
audit_date: 2026-04-19 01:30
```

### parts/v10/part03.md — §3.9 (Resolution Strategies Menu)

```yaml
file: parts/v10/part03.md
section: "§3.9 Resolution Strategies Menu (L269-301)"
parser_consumer: p_brief_builder (v2 addition)
format_summary: "Bold short code header + prose description per strategy"

  - strategy_format: "**CODE — Name.** Description paragraph."
  - strategy_codes: "IEC, SZ, TS, DAR, SRW, PP, T0, RS, OT-REF"
  - strategy_count: 9
edge_cases:
  - "OT-REF is a Tier 2 escalation, not a resolution strategy per se"
  - "No structured fields — parser must extract code, name, description from prose"
  - "Strategies are referenced by short code elsewhere in Part 3 and Part 5"
audit_status: PASS-WITH-NOTES
audit_date: 2026-04-19 01:30
notes: "Parser needs regex: **CODE — Name.** followed by paragraph text. Consistent format across all 9 entries. Brief Builder logic validation (A6) depends on this section + Part 9 §9.2.2."
```

### parts/v10/part13.md — Glossary

```yaml
file: parts/v10/part13.md
section: "## Glossary (L365-469)"
parser_consumer: p00_glossary
format_summary: "Four sub-sections with different formats"

  - disability_codes: "Markdown table | Code | Category | Notes | (19 entries, L393-415)"
  - technical_abbreviations: "Markdown table | Abbreviation | Full Term | Context | (20 entries, L421-448)"
  - design_frameworks: "Markdown table | Short Form | Full Reference | Role | (14 entries, L452-468)"
edge_cases:

  - "CO-0003 terminology changes (INTRA/INTER/BOTH) flagged as PENDING review in HTML comment at top of file"
  - "BAR note at L417 is prose, not a table row"
  - "Total glossary entries: ~62 across 4 subsections"
audit_status: PASS-WITH-NOTES
audit_date: 2026-04-19 01:30
notes: "Mixed format (prose definitions + tables). Parser needs two extraction modes: bold-definition for Core Concepts, pipe-table for the three tabular subsections. CO-0003 terminology review still PENDING per file comment."
```

## Audit 2/3 — 2026-04-19 02:07

### parts/v10/part04.md (Part 4: Item Specification Library)

```yaml
file: parts/v10/part04.md
section: full file (3352 lines, 249925 chars, ~63K tokens)
parser_consumer: p09_specifications, p18_categories
format_summary: "H3 per item (### X-NN Title). 7 bold-labelled fields per item."
known_patterns:
  - heading_format: "### X-NN Title"
  - item_count: 91 (including E-10 duplicate)
  - categories: "A(17), B(12), C(6), D(11), E(15), F(8), G(9), H(5), I(4), K(4)"
  - field_labels: "**Applicable Groups:** · **Description:** · **Specifications:** · **Retrofit cost note:** · **Key citations:** · **Cross-reference:** · **Evidence basis (OT):**"
  - field_coverage: "Applicable Groups 94/91 · Description 92/91 · Specifications 93/91 · Retrofit 84/91 · Citations 84/91 · Cross-ref 90/91 · Evidence basis 84/91"
  - con_annotations: "58/91 items have <!-- CON-NNNN --> HTML comment annotations"
  - additional_fields: "some items have **Evidence disclosure:** · **FDR-*:** · **Conflict notes:** · **Population conflict:** · **Zone cross-reference:**"
edge_cases:
  - "E-10 DUPLICATE: two entries at L1737 and L3205 with different content — merge or rename required (A4)"
  - "F-06: 5 lines only — stub item"
  - "B-04: marked [MERGED INTO B-03] but still has full content"
  - "H-05 appears between I-03 and I-04 — out of category sort order"
  - "G-08, G-09, B-12, E-12, E-13: appear after K-04 — appended late, not in category order"
  - "v2 HTML annotations (design_stage_lock, ve_risk, ot_appointment_trigger): 0/91 — Block 3 A7 task"
  - "Some items have **Design Stage:** field; others embed stage in Specifications text"
  - "CON annotations in HTML comments not in consistent schema — some have [HIGH], some [MEDIUM], some no confidence"
audit_status: PASS-WITH-NOTES
audit_date: 2026-04-19 02:07
notes: "Parser must handle: variable field presence, out-of-order items, merged/stub items, HTML comment annotations. Item index created at references/part04-item-index.md for targeted loading."
```

audit_status: PASS-WITH-NOTES
audit_date: 2026-04-19 02:07
notes: "Two Part 5 files exist. part05.md is larger and appears more current. Reconciliation required before Phase B."

file: parts/v10/part06.md

notes: "Parser must handle variable population column counts per room. ● / ○ / — markers are consistent. DAR provisions follow each room's item table."

audit_date: 2026-04-19 02:07
notes: "Part 7 uses a different matrix format than Part 6. Parser p07 needs separate extraction logic from p06. Phantom item codes (GAP-XREF-02) need resolution before Phase B."

file: parts/v10/part08.md

## A5 — Standards Registry Gap Quantification (2026-04-19 02:24)

Current standards-registry.md: **30 entries** across **15 jurisdictions** (+ 1 template entry).
Block 3 target: **≥80 entries**.
Gap: **50+ entries needed**.

BPC files do not have a dedicated jurisdiction_coverage section. Standards references are distributed across Consensus findings, Divergent findings, and NO-DATA tables. A full count requires scanning all 77 BPC files (~604K bytes). This scan deferred to Block 3 A5 (exec) when BPC files are being read for CO-0006 migration anyway — standard identifiers will be extracted during that pass.

---

## A6 — Decision Tree + OT Trigger Validation (2026-04-19 02:24)

### §3.8 Decision Tree Validation

All 11 conflicts from Part 5 §5.2 tested against §3.8 decision tree steps:

| Domain | Type | Resolution | Tree path | OT trigger? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| COLOUR-CONT | INTER-GROUP | RESOLVED-EVIDENCE | Step 3 | YES (pop) |
| ACOUSTIC-LVL | INTER-GROUP | RESOLVED-CONSENSUS | Step 4 | YES (pop) |
| GRAB-DIAM | BOTH | RESOLVED | Step 2/4 | NO |
| TEMP-RANGE | BOTH | RESOLVED-CONSENSUS | Step 4 | YES (pop) |
| SURFACE-TEXT | INTER-GROUP | PARTIAL | Step 5→6 | YES (pop) |
| SPATIAL-OPEN | INTER-GROUP | RESOLVED-CONSENSUS | Step 4 | YES (pop) |
| LIGHT-INT | INTER-GROUP | RESOLVED-CONSENSUS | Step 4 | YES (pop) |
| VIS-COMPLEX | INTER-GROUP | RESOLVED (zoning) | Step 5 | YES (pop) |
| MOVE-FREE | INTER-GROUP | RESOLVED | Step 2/4/5 | YES (pop) |
| FRAGRANCE | INTER-GROUP | PARTIAL | Step 5→6 | YES (pop) |
| DOOR-OPERATION | INTER-GROUP | RESOLVED | Step 2/4/5 | YES (pop) |

### §9.2.2 OT Trigger Coverage

**Triggers cover:** NDV, DEM, NEU, OFS, DBL as primary populations + Tier 2 projects + healthcare/supported housing.

**Findings:**
- 10 of 11 conflicts involve at least one OT-trigger population — correct coverage.
- GRAB-DIAM (MOB vs UPL) is the only conflict without an OT-trigger population — resolves at Step 2/4 without OT referral. Correct.
- PAIN is not an OT-trigger population — correct, PAIN conflicts resolve through harm-asymmetry (project-standards rule), not OT referral.
- 2 PARTIAL conflicts (SURFACE-TEXT, FRAGRANCE) route to Step 6 → §9.10 OT threshold. Both involve OT-trigger populations. Pathway verified.

### Validation result

**PASS.** Decision tree is internally consistent. OT triggers cover all Step 6 escalation paths. No missing population pairs. No contradictions between §3.8, §5.2, and §9.2.2.
